devanagari: श्रीराजोवाच । देवासुरमनुष्येसु ये भजन्त्यशिवं शिवम् । प्रायस्ते धनिनो भोजा न तु लक्ष्म्याः पतिं हरिम् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīrājovāca devāsuramanuṣyesu ye bhajantyaśivaṃ śivam prāyaste dhanino bhojā na tu lakṣmyāḥ patiṃ harim

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The King (Parīkṣit) enquired:
Out of gods, Asuras or human beings, whoever worship (and propitiate) god Śiva who himself has renounced all worldly pleasures are blessed with wealth and enjoyment of pleasures, but not so in the case of those who are the votaries of Viṣṇu though he is the consort of the goddess of wealth.

devanagari: एतद्वेदितुमिच्छामः सन्देहोऽत्र महान्हि नः । विरुद्धशीलयोः प्रभ्वोर्विरुद्धा भजतां गतिः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: etadveditumicchāmaḥ sandeho viruddhaśīlayoḥ prabhvorviruddhā bhajatāṃ gatiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single]

translation: We entertain a grave doubt and we desire to know (its solution) as to why the goal (i.e. the fruit achieved) of those who worship these two deities of different dispositions, is quite contrary to their expectation? Why do the Devotees of the Lord of Lakṣmī do not get wealth and while those of Siva become rich?

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । शिवः शक्तियुतः शश्वत्त्रिलिङ्गो गुणसंवृतः । वैकारिकस्तैजसश्च तामसश्चेत्यहं त्रिधा ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca śivaḥ śaktiyutaḥ śaśvattriliṅgo guṇasaṃvṛtaḥ vaikārikastaijasaśca tāmasaścetyahaṃ tridhā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
God Śiva is ever associated with his Śakti. He is invested with three guṇas viz. Sattva, rajas and tamas and is the presiding deity of the three types of (Cosmic) ego characterised by the guṇas, viz. sattva (or vaikārika ego, rajas (taijasa type of Ego) and tamas (or tāmasa type of ego).

devanagari: ततो विकारा अभवन्षोडशामीषु कञ्चन । उपधावन्विभूतीनां सर्वासामश्नुते गतिम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: tato vikārā abhavanṣoḍaśāmīṣu kañcana upadhāvanvibhūtīnāṃ sarvāsāmaśnute gatim

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ u class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ u class 1 verb]

translation: Out of these three types of ego were evolved sixteen modifications or products (viz. mind from the sāttvika ego, ten sense-organs from rājasic type of ahaṃkāra and five bhūtas or elements from the tāmasic type of ego). He who adores one of these potencies is blessed with all types of earthly prosperity.

devanagari: हरिर्हि निर्गुणः साक्षात्पुरुषः प्रकृतेः परः । स सर्वदृगुपद्र ष्टा तं भजन्निर्गुणो भवेत् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: harirhi nirguṇaḥ sākṣātpuruṣaḥ prakṛteḥ paraḥ sa sarvadṛgupadra ṣṭā taṃ bhajannirguṇo bhavet
translation: Lord Hari, on the other hand, is unaffected by and hence devoid of the attributes (of Prakṛti). He is the Supreme Person far beyond the range of Prakṛti. He is omniscient and is a witness to everything. A person who worships him becomes bereft of guṇas.

devanagari: निवृत्तेष्वश्वमेधेषु राजा युष्मत्पितामहः । शृण्वन्भगवतो धर्मानपृच्छदिदमच्युतम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: nivṛtteṣvaśvamedheṣu rājā yuṣmatpitāmahaḥ śṛṇvanbhagavato dharmānapṛcchadidamacyutam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ śru class 5 verb], [vocative single from √ śru class 5 verb]

translation: After the conclusion of the Horse-sacrifice, while listening to the exposition of the righteous duties, your grandfather, King Yudhiṣṭhira asked this very question to Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: स आह भगवांस्तस्मै प्रीतः शुश्रूषवे प्रभुः । नृणां निःश्रेयसार्थाय योऽवतीर्णो यदोः कुले ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: sa āha bhagavāṃstasmai prītaḥ śuśrūṣave prabhuḥ nṛṇāṃ niḥśreyasārthāya yo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ prī class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ prī class 9 verb]

translation: The glorious Lord Kṛṣṇa who descended into the race of Yadu for blessing people with the Summum bonum (by showing the way to Mokṣa) was very much pleased with that question and explained to him who was very desirous and eager to listen to him.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । यस्याहमनुगृह्णामि हरिष्ये तद्धनं शनैः । ततोऽधनं त्यजन्त्यस्य स्वजना दुःखदुःखितम् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca yasyāhamanugṛhṇāmi hariṣye taddhanaṃ śanaiḥ tato

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

[nominative dual from √ tyaj class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ tyaj class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ tyaj class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ tyaj class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ tyaj class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ tyaj class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ tyaj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ tyaj class 1 verb]

translation:
The Lord explained:
I gradually relieve a person of his wealth on whom I wish to bestow my grace. Then his own relatives, finding him penniless and hard-pressed with adversity and sorrow- stricken, forsake him (of their own accord).

devanagari: स यदा वितथोद्योगो निर्विण्णः स्याद्धनेहया । मत्परैः कृतमैत्रस्य करिष्ये मदनुग्रहम् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: sa yadā vitathodyogo nirviṇṇaḥ syāddhanehayā matparaiḥ kṛtamaitrasya kariṣye madanugraham

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative active second single]
[nominative single from √ mad class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ mad class 1 verb]

translation: When (he finds that) all his efforts with the desire of acquiring wealth have been futile, he becomes despondent and forms friendship with persons who are devoted to me. Then only I shower my grace on him.

devanagari: तद्ब्रह्म परमं सूक्ष्मं चिन्मात्रं सदनन्तकम् । विज्ञायात्मतया धीरः संसारात्परिमुच्यते ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: tadbrahma paramaṃ sūkṣmaṃ cinmātraṃ sadanantakam vijñāyātmatayā dhīraḥ saṃsārātparimucyate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sad class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ sad class 6 verb]
[instrumental single from √ man class 4 verb], [instrumental single from √ man class 8 verb]

translation:
My grace is the revelation of myself i.e. of Brahman. It is too subtle (to be comprehensible), Pure consciousness and infinite Reality (existence).
[Additional half verse from the vṛnḍāban edition (Quoted from Bhāgavata Purāṇa in Bengali script):]
10(A). The intelligent person who realizes it as his own-self is liberated from Saṃsāra. Hence, people avoid me as one too difficult to be propitiated and turn to other gods (to worship).

devanagari: अतो मां सुदुराराध्यं हित्वान्यान्भजते जनः । ततस्त आशुतोषेभ्यो लब्धराज्यश्रियोद्धताः । मत्ताः प्रमत्ता वरदान्विस्मयन्त्यवजानते ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: ato māṃ sudurārādhyaṃ hitvānyānbhajate janaḥ tatasta āśutoṣebhyo labdharājyaśriyoddhatāḥ mattāḥ pramattā varadānvismayantyavajānate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ mad class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ mad class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ mad class 3 verb], [vocative plural from √ mad class 3 verb], [nominative plural from √ mad class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ mad class 4 verb]

[nominative dual from √ i class 2 verb], [nominative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Thereupon, having obtained sovereignty and prosperity from gods who are easily and quickly propitiated, they become arrogant, puffed up with pride and maddened. They forget the very gods who have bestowed boons on them and despise them.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । शापप्रसादयोरीशा ब्रह्मविष्णुशिवादयः । सद्यः शापप्रसादोऽङ्ग शिवो ब्रह्मा न चाच्युतः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca śāpaprasādayorīśā brahmaviṣṇuśivādayaḥ sadyaḥ śāpaprasādo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Gods Brahmā, Viṣṇu, Śiva and others are competent to pronounce a curse or grant boons. But dear Parīkṣit, God Śiva and Brahmā accord grace or curse instantaneously. (The moment they are pleased or displeased), but not so god Viṣṇu.

devanagari: अत्र चोदाहरन्तीममितिहासं पुरातनम् । वृकासुराय गिरिशो वरं दत्त्वाप सङ्कटम् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: atra codāharantīmamitihāsaṃ purātanam vṛkāsurāya giriśo varaṃ dattvāpa saṅkaṭam
translation: As an illustration of this, those versed in ancient lore cite the ancient legend how god Śiva after giving a boon to Vṛkāsura, found himself in danger.

devanagari: वृको नामासुरः पुत्रः शकुनेः पथि नारदम् । दृष्ट्वाशुतोषं पप्रच्छ देवेषु त्रिषु दुर्मतिः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: vṛko nāmāsuraḥ putraḥ śakuneḥ pathi nāradam dṛṣṭvāśutoṣaṃ papraccha deveṣu triṣu durmatiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: There was an Asura called Vṛka, the son of Śakuni. That wicked-minded fellow saw Nārada on the way and asked him which god out of the trinity (of Brahmā, Viṣṇu and Śiva) was easily and quickly propitiated.

devanagari: स आह देवं गिरिशमुपाधावाशु सिद्ध्यसि । योऽल्पाभ्यां गुणदोषाभ्यामाशु तुष्यति कुप्यति ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: sa āha devaṃ giriśamupādhāvāśu siddhyasi yo
translation: Nārada advised him, “Do propitiate god Śiva and you will quickly accomplish your purpose. He gets pleased with slight merit and is enraged at the slightest offence.

devanagari: दशास्यबाणयोस्तुष्टः स्तुवतोर्वन्दिनोरिव । ऐश्वर्यमतुलं दत्त्वा तत आप सुसङ्कटम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: daśāsyabāṇayostuṣṭaḥ stuvatorvandinoriva aiśvaryamatulaṃ dattvā tata āpa susaṅkaṭam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [genitive dual from √ stu class 2 verb], [locative dual from √ stu class 2 verb]
[genitive dual from √ stu class 2 verb], [locative dual from √ stu class 2 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: When the ten-faced demon Rāvaṇa and Bāṇa eulogised him like bards, he bestowed on them unparalleled prosperity and sovereignty in consequence of which he found himself involved in serious troubles (Rāvaṇa uprooted mount Kailāsa, the residence of god śiva and Bāṇa made him the protector of his capital city).

devanagari: इत्यादिष्टस्तमसुर उपाधावत्स्वगात्रतः । केदार आत्मक्रव्येण जुह्वानो ग्निमुखं हरम् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: ityādiṣṭastamasura upādhāvatsvagātrataḥ kedāra ātmakravyeṇa juhvāno gnimukhaṃ haram
translation: Being thus advised, that Asura began to propitiate god Śiva by offering to him the oblation of his own flesh chopped from his body, through the mouth of the fire-god, at the place called Kedāra in the Himālayas.

devanagari: देवोपलब्धिमप्राप्य निर्वेदात्सप्तमेऽहनि । शिरोऽवृश्चत्सुधितिना तत्तीर्थक्लिन्नमूर्धजम् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: devopalabdhimaprāpya nirvedātsaptame śiro

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ klid class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ klid class 4 verb]

translation: Out of desparation for not being able to visualize god Śiva (in spite of the sacrifice of his flesh), the Asura, on the seventh day, (took a bath in the holy waters of that place) and began to cut with sharp weapon his head with wet matted hair.

devanagari: तदा महाकारुणिको स धूर्जटिर्यथा वयं चाग्निरिवोत्थितोऽनलात् । निगृह्य दोर्भ्यां भुजयोर्न्यवारयत्तत्स्पर्शनाद्भूय उपस्कृताकृतिः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: tadā mahākāruṇiko sa dhūrjaṭiryathā vayaṃ cāgnirivotthito nigṛhya dorbhyāṃ bhujayornyavārayattatsparśanādbhūya upaskṛtākṛtiḥ
translation: At that time the highly merciful god (with matted hair) who was brilliant like the fire-god, came out of the sacrificial fire. Just as we would do (to prevent our friends from suicide), he held both of his arms with his own and prevented him from that act. By the blessed touch of śiva, his body became healed of wounds and perfect as before.

devanagari: तमाह चाङ्गालमलं वृणीष्व मे यथाभिकामं वितरामि ते वरम् । प्रीयेय तोयेन नृणां प्रपद्यतामहो त्वयात्मा भृशमर्द्यते वृथा ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: tamāha cāṅgālamalaṃ vṛṇīṣva me yathābhikāmaṃ vitarāmi te varam prīyeya toyena nṛṇāṃ prapadyatāmaho tvayātmā bhṛśamardyate vṛthā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [optative middle first single], [optative passive first single]

translation: He spoke to him, “Dear Asura! It is enough (desist from offering your head to me). Seek of me any boon you desire and I shall confer it on you. I am pleased even if water is reverentially offered to me by people devoted to me. You have unnecessarily tortured your body so cruelly.

devanagari: देवं स वव्रे पापीयान्वरं भूतभयावहम् । यस्य यस्य करं शीर्ष्णि धास्ये स म्रियतामिति ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: devaṃ sa vavre pāpīyānvaraṃ bhūtabhayāvaham yasya yasya karaṃ śīrṣṇi dhāsye sa mriyatāmiti

[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single], [imperative passive third single]

translation: That extremely sinful demon sought the god a boon that spelt terror to all living beings. (He requested as a boon:) “May every body die on whose head I shall lay my palm.”

devanagari: तच्छ्रुत्वा भगवान्रुद्रो दुर्मना इव भारत । ॐ इति प्रहसंस्तस्मै ददेऽहेरमृतं यथा ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: tacchrutvā bhagavānrudro durmanā iva bhārata oṃ iti prahasaṃstasmai dade

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[present middle first single], [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Hearing that prayer, Lord Śiva got depressed at heart (for a moment), O Parīkṣit. But laughingly he said, “Yes. Be it so.” and gave the boon which was like giving nectar to a serpent.

devanagari: स तद्वरपरीक्षार्थं शम्भोर्मूर्ध्नि किलासुरः । स्वहस्तं धातुमारेभे सोऽबिभ्यत्स्वकृताच्छिवः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: sa tadvaraparīkṣārthaṃ śambhormūrdhni kilāsuraḥ svahastaṃ dhātumārebhe so

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single], [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: The Asura who was thus granted boon, was really desirous of laying his hand upon Gauri (Pārvatī, the spouse of god Śiva). And they say that with a view to testing the efficacy of that boon, he advanced to place his hand upon the head of god Śiva himself. And god Śiva was seized with terror at his own act (of granting the boon.)

devanagari: तेनोपसृष्टः सन्त्रस्तः पराधावन्सवेपथुः । यावदन्तं दिवो भूमेः कष्ठानामुदगादुदक् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: tenopasṛṣṭaḥ santrastaḥ parādhāvansavepathuḥ yāvadantaṃ divo bhūmeḥ kaṣṭhānāmudagādudak

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ u class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ u class 1 verb]

translation: Agitated with terrific fear and trembling, he began to run fast to the northern direction with the demon pursuing (close on his heels). He ran traversing the whole of the earth, the heaven and the end of cardinal points.

devanagari: अजानन्तः प्रतिविधिं तूष्णीमासन्सुरेश्वराः । ततो वैकुण्ठमगमद्भास्वरं तमसः परम् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: ajānantaḥ pratividhiṃ tūṣṇīmāsansureśvarāḥ tato vaikuṇṭhamagamadbhāsvaraṃ tamasaḥ param
translation: Lords of gods did not know how to counteract or intervene and they kept quiet. At last Lord Siva went to Śvetadvīpa the ever-refulgent region of Viṣṇu lying beyond the sphere of darkness.

devanagari: यत्र नारायणः साक्षान्न्यासिनां परमो गतिः । शान्तानां न्यस्तदण्डानां यतो नावर्तते गतः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: yatra nārāyaṇaḥ sākṣānnyāsināṃ paramo gatiḥ śāntānāṃ nyastadaṇḍānāṃ yato nāvartate gataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [genitive plural from √ śam class 4 verb], [genitive plural from √ śam class 9 verb]
[genitive plural from √ śam class 4 verb], [genitive plural from √ śam class 9 verb]
[genitive plural from √ śam class 4 verb], [genitive plural from √ śam class 9 verb]
[accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: There abides Lord Nārāyaṇa in person, the ultimate resort of those who have renounced everything for the Lord, and hence have attained serenity of mind (by getting rid of passions like love, hatred, etc.) and have refrained from all forms of violence, an abode having attained which a person never returns to Saṃsāra.

devanagari: तं तथा व्यसनं दृष्ट्वा भगवान्वृजिनार्दनः । दूरात्प्रत्युदियाद्भूत्वा बटुको योगमायया ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ tathā vyasanaṃ dṛṣṭvā bhagavānvṛjinārdanaḥ dūrātpratyudiyādbhūtvā baṭuko yogamāyayā
translation: Perceiving the predicament of god Śiva, the Lord who destroys all afflictions, assumed the form of a small boy by his Yoga-māyā and manifested himself at a distance.

devanagari: मेखलाजिनदण्डाक्षैस्तेजसाग्निरिव ज्वलन् । अभिवादयामास च तं कुशपाणिर्विनीतवत् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: mekhalājinadaṇḍākṣaistejasāgniriva jvalan abhivādayāmāsa ca taṃ kuśapāṇirvinītavat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ jval class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ jval class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Shining like fire by his refulgence and adorned with his girdle of muñja grass, deer-skin, staff and a rosary of Rudrākṣa beads and with darbha grass in his hand, he paid obeisance to the demon as if in deep humility.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । शाकुनेय भवान्व्यक्तं श्रान्तः किं दूरमागतः । क्षणं विश्रम्यतां पुंस आत्मायं सर्वकामधुक् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca śākuneya bhavānvyaktaṃ śrāntaḥ kiṃ dūramāgataḥ kṣaṇaṃ viśramyatāṃ puṃsa ātmāyaṃ sarvakāmadhuk

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative plural from √ śrā class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ śrā class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ śrā class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ śrā class 2 verb]

translation:
The Lord said:
O son of Śakuni, evidently your honour is much exhausted. Have you come from a long distance? Have rest for a short while (for a minute) (If proper care is taken of this body,) it fulfills all the cherished desires like the heavenly wish-yielding cow.

devanagari: यदि नः श्रवणायालं युष्मद्व्यवसितं विभो । भण्यतां प्रायशः पुम्भिर्धृतैः स्वार्थान्समीहते ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: yadi naḥ śravaṇāyālaṃ yuṣmadvyavasitaṃ vibho bhaṇyatāṃ prāyaśaḥ pumbhirdhṛtaiḥ svārthānsamīhate
translation: If the matter is fit for our ears, (i.e. if there be no objection in hearing of your intention) O mighty one, let me know on what you have set your mind. Generally a man desires to accomplish his purpose with the help of his chosen assistants.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । एवं भगवता पृष्टो वचसामृतवर्षिणा । गतक्लमोऽब्रवीत्तस्मै यथापूर्वमनुष्ठितम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca evaṃ bhagavatā pṛṣṭo vacasāmṛtavarṣiṇā gataklamo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
When he was thus accosted in such nectar-showering words, the demon felt refreshed and confided to him (Viṣṇu) all that he had done before.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । एवं चेत्तर्हि तद्वाक्यं न वयं श्रद्दधीमहि । यो दक्षशापात्पैशाच्यं प्राप्तः प्रेतपिशाचराट् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca evaṃ cettarhi tadvākyaṃ na vayaṃ śraddadhīmahi yo dakṣaśāpātpaiśācyaṃ prāptaḥ pretapiśācarāṭ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The glorious Lord (disguised as a Brahmacārin) remarked:
If this be the case, we do not believe in his (Śiva’s) words; for he has been reduced to the State of a goblin (Piśāca) by the curse of Dakṣa, and is now the king of Pretas and Piśācas (evil spirits).

devanagari: यदि वस्तत्र विश्रम्भो दानवेन्द्र जगद्गुरौ । तर्ह्यङ्गाशु स्वशिरसि हस्तं न्यस्य प्रतीयताम् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: yadi vastatra viśrambho dānavendra jagadgurau tarhyaṅgāśu svaśirasi hastaṃ nyasya pratīyatām
translation: If you regard Śiva as the preceptor of the world and still have faith in him, O king of Dānavas, you may easily test the truth of his words this very moment, O dear Asura, by placing your palm on your head.

devanagari: यद्यसत्यं वचः शम्भोः कथञ्चिद्दानवर्षभ । तदैनं जह्यसद्वाचं न यद्वक्तानृतं पुनः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: yadyasatyaṃ vacaḥ śambhoḥ kathañciddānavarṣabha tadainaṃ jahyasadvācaṃ na yadvaktānṛtaṃ punaḥ
translation: If the words of Śiva are found to be false, you straightway kill that liar so that he may not live to utter a falsehood again.”

devanagari: इत्थं भगवतश्चित्रैर्वचोभिः स सुपेशलैः । भिन्नधीर्विस्मृतः शीर्ष्णि स्वहस्तं कुमतिर्न्यधात् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: itthaṃ bhagavataścitrairvacobhiḥ sa supeśalaiḥ bhinnadhīrvismṛtaḥ śīrṣṇi svahastaṃ kumatirnyadhāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active third single], [aorist active third single]

translation: In this way his mind was changed by the wonderful and extremely soft, winning words of the Lord. And the wicked-minded fellow in utter forgetfulness placed his palm on his own head.

devanagari: अथापतद्भिन्नशिराः व्रजाहत इव क्षणात् । जयशब्दो नमःशब्दः साधुशब्दोऽभवद्दिवि ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: athāpatadbhinnaśirāḥ vrajāhata iva kṣaṇāt jayaśabdo namaḥśabdaḥ sādhuśabdo
translation: That very moment the Asura fell down with his head split up as if he was struck down with vajra (thunder-bolt). And there were applauding shouts like “Victory” “Salutations” and “Well-done” in the heavens.

devanagari: मुमुचुः पुष्पवर्षाणि हते पापे वृकासुरे । देवर्षिपितृगन्धर्वा मोचितः सङ्कटाच्छिवः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: mumucuḥ puṣpavarṣāṇi hate pāpe vṛkāsure devarṣipitṛgandharvā mocitaḥ saṅkaṭācchivaḥ

translation: When the wicked demon Vṛkāsura was killed, gods, sages, manes (ancestors) and Gandharvas let down showers of flowers and god Śiva was rescued from a dangerous situation.

devanagari: मुक्तं गिरिशमभ्याह भगवान्पुरुषोत्तमः । अहो देव महादेव पापोऽयं स्वेन पाप्मना ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: muktaṃ giriśamabhyāha bhagavānpuruṣottamaḥ aho deva mahādeva pāpo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ muc class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ muc class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ muc class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ muc class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ muj class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ muj class 1 verb]

translation: To god Śiva who was thus saved, spoke the glorious Lord Viṣṇu: God Mahādeva! This sinful fellow has been killed by his own sins. O Iśa (Controller of the world)! What creature who has committed sin (or offence) against the great can possibly be safe and happy? What need be said when the offence is committed against the controller of the universe and the father of the world like you?

devanagari: हतः को नु महत्स्वीश जन्तुर्वै कृतकिल्बिषः । क्षेमी स्यात्किमु विश्वेशे कृतागस्को जगद्गुरौ ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: hataḥ ko nu mahatsvīśa janturvai kṛtakilbiṣaḥ kṣemī syātkimu viśveśe kṛtāgasko jagadgurau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ han class 2 verb]
[present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: To god Śiva who was thus saved, spoke the glorious Lord Viṣṇu: God Mahādeva! This sinful fellow has been killed by his own sins. O Iśa (Controller of the world)! What creature who has committed sin (or offence) against the great can possibly be safe and happy? What need be said when the offence is committed against the controller of the universe and the father of the world like you?

devanagari: य एवमव्याकृतशक्त्युदन्वतः परस्य साक्षात्परमात्मनो हरेः । गिरित्रमोक्षं कथयेच्छृणोति वा विमुच्यते संसृतिभिस्तथारिभिः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: ya evamavyākṛtaśaktyudanvataḥ parasya sākṣātparamātmano hareḥ giritramokṣaṃ kathayecchṛṇoti vā vimucyate saṃsṛtibhistathāribhiḥ
translation: Whoever hears or relates this account of Liberation of god Śiva (from mortal danger) by Lord Viṣṇu, the Supreme Soul, who is the veritable sea of incomprehensible powers, becomes emancipated from Saṃsāra (the cycle of births and deaths) and becomes free from the danger of enemies as well.
